children.heading: Cybersecurity Plan
children.label: 1 Cybersecurity Plan
children.text: The term “Cybersecurity Plan” means a plan submitted by an eligible entity under subsection (e)(1).

children.label: A
children.text: State; or

children.label: B
children.text: Tribal government.

children.heading: Multi-entity group
children.label: 3 Multi-entity group
children.text: The term “multi-entity group” means a group of 2 or more eligible entities desiring a grant under this section.

children.heading: Online service
children.label: 4 Online service
children.text: The term “online service” means any internet-facing service, including a website, email, virtual private network, or custom application.

children.heading: Rural area
children.label: 5 Rural area
children.text: The term “rural area” has the meaning given the term in .section 5302 of title 49

children.heading: State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program
children.label: 6 State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program
children.text: The term “State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program” means the program established under subsection (b).

children.heading: Tribal government
children.label: 7 Tribal government
children.text: The term “Tribal government” means the recognized governing body of any Indian or Alaska Native Tribe, band, nation, pueblo, village, community, component band, or component reservation, that is individually identified (including parenthetically) in the most recent list published pursuant to .section 5131 of title 25

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is established within the Department a program to award grants to eligible entities to address cybersecurity risks and cybersecurity threats to information systems owned or operated by, or on behalf of, State, local, or Tribal governments.

children.heading: Application
children.label: 2 Application
children.text: An eligible entity desiring a grant under the State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program shall submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: c Administration
children.text: The State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program shall be administered in the same office of the Department that administers grants made under sections 604 and 605 of this title.
implement the Cybersecurity Plan of the eligible entity;
develop or revise the Cybersecurity Plan of the eligible entity;
pay expenses directly relating to the administration of the grant, which shall not exceed 5 percent of the amount of the grant;

assist with activities that address imminent cybersecurity threats, as confirmed by the Secretary, acting through the Director, to the information systems owned or operated by, or on behalf of, the eligible entity or a local government within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity; or
fund any other appropriate activity determined by the Secretary, acting through the Director.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An eligible entity applying for a grant under this section shall submit to the Secretary a Cybersecurity Plan for review in accordance with subsection (i).

children.label: i
children.text: any existing plans of the eligible entity to protect against cybersecurity risks and cybersecurity threats to information systems owned or operated by, or on behalf of, State, local, or Tribal governments; and

children.label: ii
children.text: if the eligible entity is a State, consultation and feedback from local governments and associations of local governments within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity;

children.label: i
children.text: manage, monitor, and track information systems, applications, and user accounts owned or operated by, or on behalf of, the eligible entity or, if the eligible entity is a State, local governments within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity, and the information technology deployed on those information systems, including legacy information systems and information technology that are no longer supported by the manufacturer of the systems or technology;

children.label: ii
children.text: monitor, audit, and, track network traffic and activity transiting or traveling to or from information systems, applications, and user accounts owned or operated by, or on behalf of, the eligible entity or, if the eligible entity is a State, local governments within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity;1So in original. The comma probably should not appear.1

children.label: iii
children.text: enhance the preparation, response, and resiliency of information systems, applications, and user accounts owned or operated by, or on behalf of, the eligible entity or, if the eligible entity is a State, local governments within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity, against cybersecurity risks and cybersecurity threats;

children.label: iv
children.text: implement a process of continuous cybersecurity vulnerability assessments and threat mitigation practices prioritized by degree of risk to address cybersecurity risks and cybersecurity threats on information systems, applications, and user accounts owned or operated by, or on behalf of, the eligible entity or, if the eligible entity is a State, local governments within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity;

children.label: I
children.text: the practices set forth in the cybersecurity framework developed by the National Institute of Standards and Technology;

children.label: II
children.text: cyber chain supply chain risk management best practices identified by the National Institute of Standards and Technology; and

children.label: III
children.text: knowledge bases of adversary tools and tactics;

children.label: vi
children.text: promote the delivery of safe, recognizable, and trustworthy online services by the eligible entity and, if the eligible entity is a State, local governments within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity, including through the use of the .gov internet domain;

children.label: vii
children.text: ensure continuity of operations of the eligible entity and, if the eligible entity is a State, local governments within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity, in the event of a cybersecurity incident, including by conducting exercises to practice responding to a cybersecurity incident;

children.label: viii
children.text: use the National Initiative for Cybersecurity Education Workforce Framework for Cybersecurity developed by the National Institute of Standards and Technology to identify and mitigate any gaps in the cybersecurity workforces of the eligible entity and, if the eligible entity is a State, local governments within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity, enhance recruitment and retention efforts for those workforces, and bolster the knowledge, skills, and abilities of personnel of the eligible entity and, if the eligible entity is a State, local governments within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity, to address cybersecurity risks and cybersecurity threats, such as through cybersecurity hygiene training;

children.label: ix
children.text: if the eligible entity is a State, ensure continuity of communications and data networks within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity between the eligible entity and local governments within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity in the event of an incident involving those communications or data networks;

children.label: x
children.text: assess and mitigate, to the greatest degree possible, cybersecurity risks and cybersecurity threats relating to critical infrastructure and key resources, the degradation of which may impact the performance of information systems within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity;

children.label: I
children.text: if the eligible entity is a State, local governments within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity, including by expanding information sharing agreements with the Department; and

children.label: II
children.text: the Department;

children.label: xii
children.text: leverage cybersecurity services offered by the Department;

children.label: xiii
children.text: implement an information technology and operational technology modernization cybersecurity review process that ensures alignment between information technology and operational technology cybersecurity objectives;

children.label: I
children.text: if the eligible entity is a State, local governments and associations of local governments within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity; and

children.label: aa
children.text: eligible entities that neighbor the jurisdiction of the eligible entity or, as appropriate, members of an Information Sharing and Analysis Organization; and

children.label: bb
children.text: countries that neighbor the jurisdiction of the eligible entity;

children.label: xv
children.text: ensure adequate access to, and participation in, the services and programs described in this subparagraph by rural areas within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity; and

children.label: xvi
children.text: distribute funds, items, services, capabilities, or activities to local governments under subsection (n)(2)(A), including the fraction of that distribution the eligible entity plans to distribute to rural areas under subsection (n)(2)(B);

children.label: C
children.text: assess the capabilities of the eligible entity relating to the actions described in subparagraph (B);

children.label: D
children.text: describe, as appropriate and to the extent practicable, the individual responsibilities of the eligible entity and local governments within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity in implementing the plan;

children.label: E
children.text: outline, to the extent practicable, the necessary resources and a timeline for implementing the plan; and

children.label: i
children.text: implementing the plan; and

children.label: ii
children.text: reducing cybersecurity risks to, and identifying, responding to, and recovering from cybersecurity threats to, information systems owned or operated by, or on behalf of, the eligible entity or, if the eligible entity is a State, local governments within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity.

children.label: A
children.text: consult with the Multi-State Information Sharing and Analysis Center;

children.label: B
children.text: include a description of cooperative programs developed by groups of local governments within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity to address cybersecurity risks and cybersecurity threats; and

children.label: C
children.text: include a description of programs provided by the eligible entity to support local governments and owners and operators of critical infrastructure to address cybersecurity risks and cybersecurity threats.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may award grants under this section to a multi-entity group to support multi-entity efforts to address cybersecurity risks and cybersecurity threats to information systems within the jurisdictions of the eligible entities that comprise the multi-entity group.

children.label: A
children.text: a Cybersecurity Plan that has been reviewed by the Secretary in accordance with subsection (i); and

children.label: B
children.text: a cybersecurity planning committee established in accordance with subsection (g).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A multi-entity group applying for a multi-entity grant under paragraph (1) shall submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: i
children.text: the division of responsibilities among the eligible entities that comprise the multi-entity group;

children.label: ii
children.text: the distribution of funding from the grant among the eligible entities that comprise the multi-entity group; and

children.label: iii
children.text: how the eligible entities that comprise the multi-entity group will work together to implement the Cybersecurity Plan of each of those eligible entities.

children.label: A
children.text: assist with the development, implementation, and revision of the Cybersecurity Plan of the eligible entity;

children.label: B
children.text: approve the Cybersecurity Plan of the eligible entity; and

children.label: C
children.text: assist with the determination of effective funding priorities for a grant under this section in accordance with subsections (d) and (j).

children.label: i
children.text: the eligible entity;

children.label: ii
children.text: if the eligible entity is a State, counties, cities, and towns within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity; and

children.label: iii
children.text: institutions of public education and health within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity; and

children.label: B
children.text: include, as appropriate, representatives of rural, suburban, and high-population jurisdictions.

children.heading: Cybersecurity expertise
children.label: 3 Cybersecurity expertise
children.text: Not less than one-half of the representatives of a committee established under paragraph (1) shall have professional experience relating to cybersecurity or information technology.

children.heading: Rule of construction regarding existing planning committees
children.label: 4 Rule of construction regarding existing planning committees
A
children.text: meets the requirements of this subsection; or

children.label: B
children.text: may be expanded or leveraged to meet the requirements of this subsection, including through the formation of a cybersecurity planning subcommittee.

children.heading: Rule of construction regarding control of information systems of eligible entities
children.label: 5 Rule of construction regarding control of information systems of eligible entities
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to permit a cybersecurity planning committee of an eligible entity that meets the requirements of this subsection to make decisions relating to information systems owned or operated by, or on behalf of, the eligible entity.

children.heading: Special rule for Tribal governments
children.label: h Special rule for Tribal governments
children.text: With respect to any requirement under subsection (e) or (g), the Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of the Interior and Tribal governments, may prescribe an alternative substantively similar requirement for Tribal governments if the Secretary finds that the alternative requirement is necessary for the effective delivery and administration of grants to Tribal governments under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: review the Cybersecurity Plan of the eligible entity, including any revised Cybersecurity Plans of the eligible entity; and

children.label: ii
children.text: determine that the Cybersecurity Plan reviewed under clause (i) satisfies the requirements under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Duration of determination
children.label: B Duration of determination
children.text: In the case of a determination under subparagraph (A)(ii) that a Cybersecurity Plan satisfies the requirements under paragraph (2), the determination shall be effective for the 2-year period beginning on the date of the determination.

children.label: i
children.text: determine whether the Cybersecurity Plan and any revisions continue to meet the criteria described in paragraph (2); and

children.label: ii
children.text: renew the determination if the Secretary, acting through the Director, makes a positive determination under clause (i).

children.label: A
children.text: satisfies the requirements of subsection (e)(2); and

children.label: i
children.text: the cybersecurity planning committee of the eligible entity established under subsection (g); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Chief Information Officer, the Chief Information Security Officer, or an equivalent official of the eligible entity.

children.label: i
children.text: integral to the development of the Cybersecurity Plan of the eligible entity; or

children.label: ii
children.text: necessary to assist with activities described in subsection (d)(4), as confirmed by the Director; and

children.label: B
children.text: the eligible entity will submit to the Secretary a Cybersecurity Plan for review under this subsection by .September 30, 2023

children.label: A
children.text: regulate the manner by which an eligible entity or local government improves the cybersecurity of the information systems owned or operated by, or on behalf of, the eligible entity or local government; or

children.label: i
children.text: participation in a particular Federal program; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the use of a specific product or technology.

children.heading: Limitations on uses of funds
children.label: j Limitations on uses of funds
A
children.text: to supplant State or local funds;

children.label: B
children.text: for any recipient cost-sharing contribution;

children.label: C
children.text: to pay a ransom;

children.label: D
children.text: for recreational or social purposes; or

children.label: E
children.text: for any purpose that does not address cybersecurity risks or cybersecurity threats on information systems owned or operated by, or on behalf of, the eligible entity that receives the grant or a local government within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity.

children.heading: Compliance oversight
children.label: 2 Compliance oversight
children.text: In addition to any other remedy available, the Secretary may take such actions as are necessary to ensure that a recipient of a grant under this section uses the grant for the purposes for which the grant is awarded.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 3 Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in paragraph (1)(A) shall be construed to prohibit the use of funds from a grant under this section awarded to a State, local, or Tribal government for otherwise permissible uses under this section on the basis that the State, local, or Tribal government has previously used State, local, or Tribal funds to support the same or similar uses.

children.heading: Opportunity to amend applications
children.label: k Opportunity to amend applications
children.text: In considering applications for grants under this section, the Secretary shall provide applicants with a reasonable opportunity to correct any defects in those applications before making final awards, including by allowing applicants to revise a submitted Cybersecurity Plan.

children.label: A
children.text: 0.25 percent of such amounts to each of American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, Guam, and the United States Virgin Islands;

children.label: B
children.text: 1 percent of such amounts to each of the remaining States; and

children.label: C
children.text: 3 percent of such amounts to Tribal governments.

children.label: A
children.text: 50 percent of such remainder in the ratio that the population of each State, bears to the population of all States; and

children.label: B
children.text: 50 percent of such remainder in the ratio that the population of each State that resides in rural areas, bears to the population of all States that resides in rural areas.

children.heading: Apportionment among Tribal governments
children.label: 3 Apportionment among Tribal governments
children.text: In determining how to apportion amounts to Tribal governments under paragraph (1)(C), the Secretary shall consult with the Secretary of the Interior and Tribal governments.

children.heading: Multi-entity grants
children.label: 4 Multi-entity grants
children.text: An amount received from a multi-entity grant awarded under subsection (f)(1) by a State or Tribal government that is a member of the multi-entity group shall qualify as an apportionment for the purpose of this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: for fiscal year 2022, 90 percent;

children.label: ii
children.text: for fiscal year 2023, 80 percent;

children.label: iii
children.text: for fiscal year 2024, 70 percent; and

children.label: iv
children.text: for fiscal year 2025, 60 percent; and

children.label: i
children.text: for fiscal year 2022, 100 percent;

children.label: ii
children.text: for fiscal year 2023, 90 percent;

children.label: iii
children.text: for fiscal year 2024, 80 percent; and

children.label: iv
children.text: for fiscal year 2025, 70 percent.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may waive or modify the requirements of paragraph (1) if an eligible entity or multi-entity group demonstrates economic hardship.

children.heading: Guidelines
children.label: B Guidelines
children.text: The Secretary shall establish and publish guidelines for determining what constitutes economic hardship for the purposes of this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: changes in rates of unemployment in the jurisdiction from previous years;

children.label: ii
children.text: changes in the percentage of individuals who are eligible to receive benefits under the supplemental nutrition assistance program established under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 ( et seq.) from previous years; and7 U.S.C. 2011

children.label: iii
children.text: any other factors the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.heading: Waiver for Tribal governments
children.label: 3 Waiver for Tribal governments
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (2), the Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of the Interior and Tribal governments, may waive or modify the requirements of paragraph (1) for 1 or more Tribal governments if the Secretary determines that the waiver is in the public interest.

children.label: A
children.text: for the purpose for which the grant is awarded; and

children.label: B
children.text: in compliance with subsections (d) and (j).

children.heading: Availability of funds to local governments and rural areas
children.label: 2 Availability of funds to local governments and rural areas
i
children.text: not less than 80 percent of funds available under the grant;

children.label: ii
children.text: with the consent of the local governments, items, services, capabilities, or activities having a value of not less than 80 percent of the amount of the grant; or

children.label: iii
children.text: with the consent of the local governments, grant funds combined with other items, services, capabilities, or activities having the total value of not less than 80 percent of the amount of the grant.

children.label: i
children.text: 25 percent of the amount of the grant awarded to the eligible entity;

children.label: ii
children.text: items, services, capabilities, or activities having a value of not less than 25 percent of the amount of the grant awarded to the eligible entity; or

children.label: iii
children.text: grant funds combined with other items, services, capabilities, or activities having the total value of not less than 25 percent of the grant awarded to the eligible entity.

children.label: i
children.text: any grant awarded under this section that solely supports activities that are integral to the development or revision of the Cybersecurity Plan of the eligible entity; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, Guam, the United States Virgin Islands, or a Tribal government.

children.heading: Certifications regarding distribution of grant funds to local governments
children.label: 3 Certifications regarding distribution of grant funds to local governments
children.text: An eligible entity or multi-entity group shall certify to the Secretary that the eligible entity or multi-entity group has made the distribution to local governments required under paragraph (2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An eligible entity or multi-entity group may request in writing that the Secretary extend the period of time specified in paragraph (2) for an additional period of time.

children.heading: Approval
children.label: B Approval
children.text: The Secretary may approve a request for an extension under subparagraph (A) if the Secretary determines the extension is necessary to ensure that the obligation and expenditure of grant funds align with the purpose of the State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program.

children.heading: Direct funding
children.label: 5 Direct funding
children.text: If an eligible entity does not make a distribution to a local government required under paragraph (2) in a timely fashion, the local government may petition the Secretary to request the Secretary to provide funds directly to the local government.

children.heading: Limitation on construction
children.label: 6 Limitation on construction
children.text: A grant awarded under this section may not be used to acquire land or to construct, remodel, or perform alterations of buildings or other physical facilities.

children.heading: Consultation in allocating funds
children.label: 7 Consultation in allocating funds
children.text: An eligible entity applying for a grant under this section shall agree to consult the Chief Information Officer, the Chief Information Security Officer, or an equivalent official of the eligible entity in allocating funds from a grant awarded under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: terminate or reduce the amount of a grant awarded under this section to the eligible entity; or

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of an eligible entity that is a State, directly to the appropriate local government as a replacement grant in an amount determined by the Secretary; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of an eligible entity that is a Tribal government, to another Tribal government or Tribal governments as a replacement grant in an amount determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Consultation with State, local, and Tribal representatives
children.label: o Consultation with State, local, and Tribal representatives

children.text: guidance for applicants for grants under this section, including guidance for Cybersecurity Plans;
the study of risk-based formulas required under subsection (q)(4);
the development of guidelines required under subsection (m)(2)(B); and
any modifications described in subsection (q)(2)(D).

children.heading: Notification to Congress
children.label: p Notification to Congress
children.text: Not later than 3 business days before the date on which the Department announces the award of a grant to an eligible entity under this section, including an announcement to the eligible entity, the Secretary shall provide to the appropriate congressional committees notice of the announcement.

children.label: i
children.text: implementing the Cybersecurity Plan of the eligible entity; and

children.label: ii
children.text: reducing cybersecurity risks to, and identifying, responding to, and recovering from cybersecurity threats to, information systems owned or operated by, or on behalf of, the eligible entity or, if the eligible entity is a State, local governments within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity.

children.label: i
children.text: develop or revise a Cybersecurity Plan; or

children.label: ii
children.text: assist with the activities described in subsection (d)(4).

children.label: A
children.text: the use of grants awarded under this section;

children.label: B
children.text: the proportion of grants used to support cybersecurity in rural areas;

children.label: C
children.text: the effectiveness of the State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program;

children.label: D
children.text: any necessary modifications to the State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program; and

children.label: i
children.text: developing, implementing, or revising Cybersecurity Plans; and

children.label: ii
children.text: reducing cybersecurity risks to, and identifying, responding to, and recovering from cybersecurity threats to, information systems owned or operated by, or on behalf of, State, local, or Tribal governments as a result of the award of grants under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Director, shall make each report submitted under paragraph (2) publicly available, including by making each report available on the website of the Agency.

children.heading: Redactions
children.label: B Redactions
children.text: In making each report publicly available under subparagraph (A), the Director may make redactions that the Director, in consultation with each eligible entity, determines necessary to protect classified or other information exempt from disclosure under (commonly referred to as the “Freedom of Information Act”).section 552 of title 5

children.label: i
children.text: potential components that could be included in a risk-based formula, including the potential impact of those components on support for rural areas under this section;

children.label: ii
children.text: potential sources of data and information necessary for the implementation of a risk-based formula;

children.label: iii
children.text: any obstacles to implementing a risk-based formula, including obstacles that require a legislative solution;

children.label: iv
children.text: if a risk-based formula were to be implemented for fiscal year 2026, a recommended risk-based formula for the State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program; and

children.label: v
children.text: any other information that the Secretary, acting through the Director, determines necessary to help Congress understand the progress towards, and obstacles to, implementing a risk-based formula.

children.heading: Inapplicability of Paperwork Reduction Act
children.label: B Inapplicability of Paperwork Reduction Act
children.text: The requirements of chapter 35 of title 44 (commonly referred to as the “Paperwork Reduction Act”), shall not apply to any action taken to carry out this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: describes the cybersecurity needs of Tribal governments, which shall be determined in consultation with the Secretary of the Interior and Tribal governments; and

children.label: B
children.text: includes any recommendations for addressing the cybersecurity needs of Tribal governments, including any necessary modifications to the State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program to better serve Tribal governments.

children.label: A
children.text: the grant selection process of the Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: a sample of grants awarded under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: for fiscal year 2022, $200,000,000;

children.label: B
children.text: for fiscal year 2023, $400,000,000;

children.label: C
children.text: for fiscal year 2024, $300,000,000; and

children.label: D
children.text: for fiscal year 2025, $100,000,000.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: During a fiscal year, the Secretary or the head of any component of the Department that administers the State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program may transfer not more than 5 percent of the amounts appropriated pursuant to paragraph (1) or other amounts appropriated to carry out the State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program for that fiscal year to an account of the Department for salaries, expenses, and other administrative costs incurred for the management, administration, or evaluation of this section.

children.heading: Additional appropriations
children.label: B Additional appropriations
children.text: Any funds transferred under subparagraph (A) shall be in addition to any funds appropriated to the Department or the components described in subparagraph (A) for salaries, expenses, and other administrative costs.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), the requirements of this section shall terminate on .September 30, 2026

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: The reporting requirements under subsection (q) shall terminate on the date that is 1 year after the date on which the final funds from a grant under this section are expended or returned.
